title: Cuis clone
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Organization
|image=[[File:Stormtrooper_Saber_art.jpg]]

|type=

|leader=[[Sheyvan]]<ref name="2EdgedSword">{{InsiderCite|85|A Two-Edged Sword}}</ref>

}}
{{Quote|I'm impressed by how much the clones have retained of his Force abilities.|Emperor Palpatine|<ref name="2EdgedSword" />}}
The '''Cuis clones''' were six prototype [[clone stormtrooper]]s, bred from the [[DNA/Legends|genome]] of [[Sa Cuis]], a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] who served as one of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|personal assassins]]. Through their [[Clone template/Legends|progenitor]], they gained a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|strong connection]] to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], thus making them a unique batch compared to the rest of the [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] in the [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]], all of whom were [[Cloning/Legends|cloned]] from the templates of ordinary [[Human/Legends|Humans]] at the time. Their creation and training were closely monitored by the Emperor and his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], as part of an experiment to create a [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] of obedient [[Clone stormtrooper|clone soldiers]] trained in the ways of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. In addition to his connection to the Force, Cuis was chosen as a template by Vader because of his sense of loyalty—a trait that the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] hoped the clones would inherit from the [[Death/Legends|deceased]] Dark [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]].

==History==
===Origin===
{{Quote|His clones won't have his memories, but I'm confident they'll have the same courage and loyalty, and their ideals will simply be the ones I provide for them. Loyalty to the Emperor. And his Force powers will be exceptionally valuable in the field.|Darth Vader|<ref name="IHI" />}}
[[File:CTSaber1.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A Cuis clone's lightsaber hilt]]
In the [[Standard month/Legends|months]] following the birth of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="ROTS">{{Film|III}}</ref> the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] authorized an experiment to [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] the [[DNA/Legends|genetic code]] of a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Clone template/Legends|template]]. Instead of relying on the [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] method that created the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] entrusted the project to the [[Clone Master/Legends|Clone Masters]] of [[Arkanian Microtechnologies]], a company known for their use of [[Spaarti cloning cylinder]]s. The genome itself was the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Sa Cuis]], an [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]] who attempted to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] Vader—as part of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] Lord's [[Sith training|training]]—on Palpatine's order. Impressed by the Hand's [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|fighting skills]] and [[Force power/Legends|Force abilities]], Vader captured Cuis after defeating him in [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|combat]]. When his [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] collected enough genetic samples from Cuis for replication purposes, Vader [[Sai cha/Legends|decapitated]] his [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]].<ref name="IHI" />

Prior to his [[Death/Legends|death]], Cuis refused to admit that the one who ordered the Dark Lord's death was Vader's own [[Sith Master/Legends|master]], although Vader had already deduced that Palpatine sent Cuis to kill him as a test in his [[Sith training|training]]. Far from being vexed by Cuis's lack of cooperation, Vader respected his clear sense of duty as an Emperor's Hand, something Cuis had been unwilling to break even with the promise of being spared from execution. It was his hope that the clones of Sa Cuis would not only retain their progenitor's connection to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], but also his other qualities such as loyalty and courage.<ref name="IHI" />

===Growth and training===
{{Quote|We could simply clone an entire army of the Cuis template. We know what they can do.|Darth Vader|<ref name="2EdgedSword" />}}
[[File:V_sparring_C_clone.jpg|thumb|right|200px|A Cuis clone demonstrates his skills in lightsaber combat against Darth Vader.]]
After more than a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] of replicating the Cuis genome, Arkanian Microtechnologies had produced six prototype clones of Sa Cuis, fully grown and trained as [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. Although they wore the same [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|standard armor]] as the [[Clone stormtrooper|clone infantry]] units of the [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]], the Cuis clones were armed with a [[red]]-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] instead of the [[E-11 blaster rifle/Legends|E-11 blaster rifle]]. In addition to their skills in melee combat, the clones also learned to harness their inherited Force powers, enabling them to utilize [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinetic techniques]]. After their maturation stage was completed, the Cuis clones received their training in an [[Yinchorr training center|Imperial training center]] located on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]]. Both Emperor Palpatine and [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader closely monitored the development of the Cuis clones, but most of their practical instruction was handled by another Hand named [[Sheyvan]].<ref name="2EdgedSword" />

The two Sith Lords personally oversaw the Cuis clones during the final stage of their trials. They demonstrated their lightsaber skills against each other, as well as Darth Vader, while the Emperor observed their performance. The final test in their training was a match that pitted each of the six Cuis clones against a clone of [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Erv Lekauf]], one of Vader's most trusted [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]]. The Cuis clones were disarmed of their lightsabers given that the contest was a non-lethal exercise, although they quickly proved that their Force abilities were more than sufficient in defeating the clones of an ordinary template. The Emperor was sufficiently pleased with the results of his new Force-sensitive stormtroopers and instructed Vader to place an order with the Arkanians for an entire [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] of Cuis clones. As the Sith Lords departed Yinchorr for [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]], the Cuis clones accompanied them as the newest members of Darth Vader's [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]].<ref name="2EdgedSword" />

===Insurrection===
{{Quote|We're loyal to the man who trained us, Lord Vader.|A Cuis clone, to Darth Vader|<ref name="2EdgedSword" />}}
[[File:Sheyvan.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Sheyvan, leader of the Cuis clones, attacks Darth Vader.]]
While Palpatine and Darth Vader speculated on the advantages of cloning an entire [[Army/Legends|army]] from the Cuis template during their return to the Imperial [[Capital/Legends|capital]], Sheyvan rebelled against the Emperor, revealing the traitorous intentions he had been harboring since his discovery of Palpatine's other Hand, Sa Cuis. Now aware that he was not the only Dark Jedi assassin in the Emperor's employ as he had been led to believe, Sheyvan felt that his loyalty had been repaid with deception. In his time as the personal trainer of the Cuis clones, the Hand secretly co-opted their allegiance, ensuring that they would fight for the one who trained them, rather than the two Sith Lords that merely commissioned their creation.<ref name="2EdgedSword" />

Their [[Skirmish aboard ST 321|insurrection]] came as a great surprise to the Imperials; with the exception of the Emperor, whom Vader observed to be suspiciously calm given the situation, all of the personnel aboard Palpatine's [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttle]] were caught completely off guard and were unable to prevent the Cuis clones from seizing control of much of the [[Shuttle/Legends|vessel]]. Yet despite their demonstrated superior skills against non-Force-wielders, both during training exercises and Sheyvan's rebellion, none of the Cuis clones were able to defeat Vader in combat. The Dark Lord, with the aid of Lekauf's clone stormtroopers, engaged the ex-Hand and Cuis's clones in combat. Armed with [[Flamethrower/Legends|flamethrowers]], the Lekauf clones burned four Cuis clones; the remaining two were Force choked by Vader. Sheyvan fought Vader alone in a brief duel, but was ultimately killed when he was cut in half from shoulder to hip. Afterward, Vader and Palpatine had the Sa Cuis template decommissioned, although the latter remained open to the possibility of cloning a Force-sensitive army in the future.<ref name="2EdgedSword" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|You see, the other thing no one has ever managed to copy is Force sensitivity. Worse than that, it actually gets in the way of the cloning process. We don't know why. It just does.|Ni-Ke-Vanz, to a [[Subject 1138|renegade clone]] of the original Starkiller|<ref name="TFU2n">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II'' novelization]]</ref>}}
Because of the Cuis clones' part in Sheyvan's treachery, Darth Vader became staunchly opposed to further use of the Cuis line, as well as any Forceful template. The failed insurrection, however brief, convinced him that Force-sensitives—and even clones of Force-sensitives—were naturally inclined to secure greater power through betrayal of their masters, especially if they relied on the dark side of the Force. As such, he chose to restrict clone lines to ordinary candidates; the Stormtrooper Corps continued its use of clone soldiers, but none after the Cuis clones were engineered from a Forceful genome.<ref name="2EdgedSword" />

Though Vader had long since abandoned the concept of "Force-sensitive stormtroopers,"<ref name="2EdgedSword" /> he was forced to reconsider his position against cloning a Forceful template after the untimely death of his former [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], the pseudo Sith-turned-[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Galen Marek]].<ref name="TFU2n" /> Using the apprentice's preserved corpse as a template in the [[Timira City]] Cloning Facility on [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]],<ref name="DT">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II]]'': Distant Thunder cutscenes</ref> Vader conducted an experiment to create the perfect clone of his fallen disciple—a more powerful and obedient version of the original Starkiller.<ref name="TFU2vg">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II]]''</ref> Unlike the success Vader had enjoyed with creating stable clones from the Cuis line,<ref name="2EdgedSword" /> the Marek template proved almost impossible to replicate without encountering a myriad of setbacks, forcing Vader to repeat the procedure numerous times in an attempt to gradually correct the errors in the experiment.<ref name="Databank">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II]]'': Databank</ref> Scientists were perplexed in trying to understand why there was such an inherent difficulty in cloning a Forceful being, although a few experts—such as [[Ni-Ke-Vanz]], a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Medic/Legends|medic]]—speculated that the Force was somehow responsible for undermining the stability of clones created from genetic donors with high concentrations of [[Midi-chlorian/Legends|midi-chlorians]] in their cells.<ref name="TFU2n" />

The genetic flaws aside, the [[Aberrant clone|Marek clones]]<ref name="Databank" /> differed from their Cuis precursors<ref name="2EdgedSword" /> in another way—they were all trained by [[memory flash]]es, enabling them to inherit their donor's skills, but also Marek's emotional attachments that Vader held responsible for weakening his former apprentice.<ref name="TFU2n" /> With the exception of a [[Dark Apprentice|lone test subject]], every clone of Marek was unable to differentiate his own thoughts and memories from those that belonged to their progenitor, leading to cases of identity crises and ultimately [[Clone madness|insanity]].<ref name="Databank" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:ST_Lightsaber.png|thumb|right|200px|Original concept art of lightsaber-wielding stormtroopers]]
In the commentaries for the [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|Special Edition]], [[George Lucas]] notes that in his early conceptions of the saga, lightsabers were commonplace weapons throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], but that he later decided that they should be limited to the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Based on Lucas' original conception, [[Ralph McQuarrie]] created concept art of lightsaber-wielding stormtroopers for [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']]. This was referenced in the image above in the pose of the stormtrooper to the right of Darth Vader.
